description_long: This is a stone church with a tall, square bell tower topped by a steep, dark spire. The main body of the church features a pitched red-tiled roof, with smaller annexes and buttresses along the sides. The walls are constructed from gray stone blocks, giving the building a robust and historic appearance. Narrow, arched windows are visible along the nave, though no stained glass is apparent. The entrance is located at the base of the bell tower, marked by a small arched doorway. The overall design suggests a traditional European church, likely from the Gothic or Romanesque period.
description: A historic stone church with a tall bell tower, red-tiled roof, and arched windows, exuding a classic European architectural style.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the tall bell tower with its pointed spire and the main entrance at the base, as well as the long nave with red-tiled roofing.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the length of the nave, the pitched roof, and the rear apse, with the bell tower receding into the background.
description_view_2: The side view shows the full expanse of the red-tiled roof, the stone buttresses, and the rear section of the church.
description_view_3: This perspective focuses on the bell tower's height and the main entrance, with the nave and annexes visible along the side.
